title: As a reviewer, I want Income and Name Alignment Metadata AI guidelines that distinguish correct metadata alignment, mismatches, and unsupported cases, so that scoring is consistent
description:
Feature: Income and Name Alignment Metadata AI guidelines for Suco - Home Improvement

Scenario: Income and name metadata align with supported call evidence
Given a transcript where annual income and the relevant speaker name are stated or confirmed and the metadata matches the supported result
When the reviewer evaluates the conversation
Then the score should be Yes

Scenario: Income or name metadata conflicts with the call or income was guessed or suggested
Given a transcript where the metadata conflicts with the supported answer or the agent guessed, suggested, or populated income without a direct answer
When the reviewer evaluates the conversation
Then the score should be No

Scenario: The metadata cannot be supported from the call
Given a transcript where the required fields are blank or there is no transcript support for one or both fields
When the reviewer evaluates the conversation
Then the score should be NA

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Resolved the score from the provided Plexus version URL, reused the prior rough guidelines text as source material, and am converting it into a validated Yes/No/NA classifier document before pushing a new version.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Validated the new Income and Name Alignment Metadata AI guidelines with skills/plexus-guidelines/validate_guidelines.py and pushed them to Plexus as score version 56ee7281-2ac2-4dca-b5e0-ab228fa3e522. The new version is not champion-promoted; this change adds the missing structured guidelines document.
